title: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste

seo.title: Doenjang Jjigae Recipe: Authentic Korean Soybean Stew
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Doenjang Jjigae, a comforting Korean soybean paste stew. This easy recipe brings the rich flavors of Korea to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Doenjang Jjigae Recipe: Korean Soybean Paste Stew
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Doenjang Jjigae
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Doenjang Jjigae, a traditional Korean soybean paste stew with tofu, vegetables, and mushrooms.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Doenjang Jjigae, bursting with rich Korean flavors and wholesome ingredients.

description: Doenjang Jjigae is a beloved Korean stew, renowned for its deeply savory and comforting flavors derived from fermented soybean paste. This hearty dish is packed with an array of vegetables, tofu, and sometimes meat or seafood, simmered together in a rich, spicy broth. It's a staple at Korean tables, perfect for warming up on a chilly day or enjoying as a flavorful accompaniment to rice and other banchan.

ingredients.notes: optional, for mild heat and depth

ingredients.item: Green chili pepper (e.g., Cheongyang or Serrano)

instructions.title: Prepare the Anchovy-Kelp Broth
instructions.text: In a medium pot or ttukbaegi (earthenware pot), combine the dried anchovies, dried kelp, and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Remove and discard the anchovies and kelp. This creates a flavorful base for your stew.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables and Flavorings
instructions.text: While the broth is simmering, prepare your vegetables. Mince the garlic. Dice the onion and potato. Slice the zucchini into half-moon shapes. If using, slice the green chili pepper and chop the scallions. Cube the tofu and trim the base of the enoki mushrooms, separating them.

instructions.title: Create the Doenjang Base
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the doenjang (soybean paste) and gochujang (if using) with a few tablespoons of the prepared anchovy broth. Mix well until a smooth paste forms. This helps to dissolve the pastes evenly into the stew and prevents lumps.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer Initial Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the minced garlic, diced onion, and diced potato to the prepared broth. Bring the stew back to a gentle boil. Once boiling, stir in the doenjang-gochujang mixture until fully incorporated. Reduce the heat to medium and simmer for about 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.

instructions.title: Add Remaining Vegetables and Tofu
instructions.text: Stir in the sliced zucchini and green chili pepper (if using) into the stew. Add the cubed tofu. Continue to simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the zucchini to cook through but still retain a slight bite.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, add the enoki mushrooms and chopped scallions to the pot. Simmer for just 2-3 more minutes, as mushrooms cook quickly. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary; you might add a bit more doenjang or a pinch of salt. Drizzle with sesame oil if desired. Serve immediately with a bowl of hot rice and various banchan.

tips: For a deeper, more robust flavor, use good quality, aged doenjang. Some Koreans also like to add a pinch of ground red chili flakes (gochugaru) for extra heat and color.

If you dont have dried anchovies and kelp, you can use vegetable broth or water with a dash of soy sauce as a quick substitute for the broth base. For a richer stew, consider adding thin slices of pork belly or small clams with the potatoes.

Doenjang Jjigae is best served hot and fresh, directly from the pot (especially a ttukbaegi) to the table. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheated gently on the stovetop.
article: Doenjang Jjigae Soybean Paste is a beloved Korean Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

1/2 block (7 oz) Firm or silken tofu
